description:
Produce docs/SPEC-lifecycle-sync-participants.md as the normative reference for the rest of bl-b7fb. Foundational: implementation children cite this SPEC and rely on it to settle the contract before code lands.

## Required sections

- The participant model: what a participant is, what it declares (event subscriptions, field projection, merge function, failure policy), and why git-remote sync and external-tracker plugins are instances of the same abstraction.
- The negotiation primitive: propose -> detect-conflict -> merge -> retry, parameterized over wire protocol. Specify the conflict-detection contract (non-FF for git, plugin-emitted conflict for trackers) and the retry budget.
- Field projection model: how a participant declares which Task fields it owns authoritatively versus reads, and how merge composes projections so non-overlapping ontologies (closed-in-git vs open-in-Jira) merge without operator intervention.
- Failure policy: required (operation aborts), best-effort (warn and continue), gating (stage for human approval). Per-event, layered via config inheritance.
- Config inheritance reused from bl-2148: state-branch -> local repo -> per-invocation flag. Document the precedence rules and how legacy sync_on_change maps in.
- Backward-compat shim semantics: how legacy push/sync plugins map onto the participant interface. Defaults must reproduce today's observable behavior exactly.
- Migration plan: ordering of the implementation children, what observable behavior changes when, and the version at which the unified path becomes the default.

## Non-goals

- New plugin discovery mechanisms (still balls-plugin-{name} on PATH).
- Server-side enforcement (the bl-2148 stance applies: soft policy, hard primitives).
- Custom Status enum extensions (canonical state stays the five-variant enum; remote-side state lives in projections).

## Reference precedent

docs/SPEC-orphan-branch-state.md is the format/tone reference. Match its level of normativity.
title: SPEC: lifecycle-sync participant model
